Selected Work

Fintech · Mobile App

Nexapay

Payments & Account Management

A live fintech platform designed to make financial access feel straightforward. The hardest part: helping users make a real financial decision that had compliance requirements behind it, without making it feel like a government form.

SaaS · AI Platform

Boulote

Two-sided platforms are hard. Boulote uses AI to connect professionals and companies — my job was to design the admin experience so clearly that the AI's decisions felt transparent, not mysterious.

AI featuresTwo-sidedAdmin dashboardSaaS

How I Work

My Design Process

Not a checklist. A way of thinking I've developed over two years of working on real products — and refined every time something didn't go as planned.

process
01
Understand

Understand the Brief

Before anything else, I sit with the brief. What is this actually trying to solve? Who is it for? What does success look like — for the user and for the business? I ask questions until I can articulate the problem in my own words. If I can't explain it simply, I don't understand it well enough yet.

02
Research

Research & Competitive Analysis

Has this problem been solved before? I look at what exists — competing products, similar patterns, analogous industries — and ask: what did they do, how did they do it, and what gaps remain? I use AI as part of this process to go deeper faster, but I verify and synthesise everything myself. Findings get documented — clearly, for the client and for me.

03
Define

Define the Gaps & Opportunities

Research without direction is just information. I synthesise what I've found into a clear point of view — what the real problem is, where the opportunity lives, and what constraints I'm designing within. This becomes the foundation that every design decision gets tested against.

04
Design

Wireframe → High-Fidelity → Prototype

I move from rough structure to polished detail deliberately — not rushing to high-fidelity before the flows are right. Wireframes to test logic. High-fidelity to test feeling. Prototypes to test both together. Each stage gets documented and shared with stakeholders before moving forward.

05
Deliver & Iterate

Deliver, Test, Feedback, Repeat

Handoff is not the end. I stay close to the build process, clarify decisions for engineering, and review what's implemented against what was designed. After launch, I pay attention to how real users interact with the product — and I'm ready to go back to step one when the feedback calls for it.

About Me

I design with curiosity
and a lot of questions.

I'm a self-taught UI/UX designer based in Lagos, Nigeria. My background is in Food Science & Technology — which sounds unrelated, but it gave me something useful: a habit of working from evidence. In that degree, you don't guess. You design an experiment, collect data, analyse it, and draw conclusions. I do the same thing before I design a screen.

Over the past two years I've worked on live products in fintech, scheduling, luxury branding, and AI-powered talent platforms. Each one taught me something different about how people interact with digital products — and how often the real problem isn't the one you were originally briefed on.

I graduated with a 4.30 CGPA and I'm currently open to remote roles where design is taken seriously and the work actually ships.

"I research before I sketch.
Every single time."

let's work together ✦
